title: Workflow Automation Tools
description: JSON Schema definitions for workflow automation tools available to the ideas-brillantes assistant. Enables creating, scheduling, running, and managing multi-step automated workflows with trigger-based or cron-based execution.

tools.name: create_workflow
tools.description: Define a new automation workflow with a trigger and a sequence of action nodes.

name.description: Human-readable name for the workflow (e.g. 'Daily backup', 'Send weekly report').

trigger.description: Event or condition that starts the workflow.

config.description: Trigger-specific configuration (e.g. cron expression for 'schedule', watched path for 'file_change').

nodes.description: Ordered list of action nodes to execute when the workflow runs.

id.description: Unique identifier for this node within the workflow.

tool.description: Name of the tool to invoke at this step (e.g. 'exec_command', 'send_email').

params.description: Parameters to pass to the tool. Values may reference outputs of earlier nodes using {{node_id.output_field}} syntax.

on_error.description: Behavior when this node fails. Defaults to 'stop'.

tools.name: schedule_task
tools.description: Attach a cron-based schedule to an existing workflow so it runs automatically.

workflow_id.description: ID of the workflow to schedule.

cron_expression.description: Standard cron expression defining the schedule (e.g. '0 9 * * 1-5' for weekdays at 09:00).

tools.name: run_workflow
tools.description: Manually trigger a workflow to run immediately, optionally passing runtime inputs.

workflow_id.description: ID of the workflow to run.

inputs.description: Optional key-value map of runtime inputs made available to workflow nodes.

tools.name: list_workflows
tools.description: List all defined workflows, optionally filtered by status.

status.description: Filter workflows by their current status. Defaults to 'all'.

tools.name: delete_workflow
tools.description: Permanently delete a workflow definition and cancel any associated scheduled runs.

workflow_id.description: ID of the workflow to delete.

tools.name: get_workflow_logs
tools.description: Retrieve execution logs for a workflow, ordered most-recent first.

workflow_id.description: ID of the workflow whose logs to retrieve.

limit.description: Maximum number of log entries to return. Defaults to 50.
